75109.subject: Claude joined your Slack team.
75109.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/claude-joined-your-slack-team-genai-works-vyqoe?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BDMRGxbNnSUae9qo%2Fq4tK0A%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2dpal38eX6jIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mquxacsd~bg-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mquxacsd-bg
  
Welcome back! This edition looks at where AI is actually landing inside business, in the tools teams already use every day: Slack channels, Excel sheets and monthly software bills. Anthropic brought Claude into Slack as a teammate. Microsoft is turning Excel into a serious AI workspace for finance. A Tokyo startup showed you can challenge frontier labs without building a frontier model. And flat-rate AI pricing is starting to disappear. Four stories, one throughline: AI is becoming a complete infrastructure that companies build on. In today's Atlas Newsletter: Claude joins your Slack team: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, which lets teams tag the AI like a coworker and hand it tasks asynchronously.  Excel becomes AI's finance test: Microsoft's latest Copilot in Excel update is built to clear the bar finance has always demanded: show its work, use trusted data and trace every calculation.  Sakana's bet against giant models: The Tokyo startup, founded by a co-author of the original Transformer paper, launched Fugu, an architecture that orchestrates a team of smaller expert models behind one API instead of scaling a single giant one.  Your flat AI plan is disappearing: Agents burn far more compute than chatbots, since one task can trigger dozens of model calls, and flat fees no longer cover the bill.  PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. Anthropic injects a coworker inside your Slack channel Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a way to make its AI assistant available directly in Slack. So teams can tag it like a teammate, hand it tasks asynchronously and let it build context across channels, codebases and tools over time. Where Claude Code brought agentic capability to individuals, Claude Tag brought it to teams.  You tag @Claude with a task in a channel, and the AI breaks it into stages, works through them using the tools and data it's approved for, then responds when it's done.  It learns as it goes, builds context about the work, and can act across different channels, but only the ones it has access to.  There's also an ambient mode, where Claude pulls information from relevant channels on its own and follows up on tasks that have gone idle and may need attention. The identity design is the part worth pausing on.  In a single-player AI setup, you connect your

75107.subject: 📉 Bitcoin craters as Asian markets trigger circuit breakers
75107.body:
Today’s newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick O’Neill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger you’ll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure you’re subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

———————————————————————————

## Top Stories

**BTC** $59,337 (-2.9%) | **ETH** $1,545 (-5.1%) | **SOL** $69 (0.8%)

* US Government forces slow rollout of OpenAI’s GPT5.6 due to [“Mythos-like” capabilities](https://x.com/AndrewCurran_/status/2070316740127650061?s=20) — OpenAI executives push to [delay IPO](https://x.com/zoomerfied/status/2070245069634895977?s=20) to next year

* Apple has worst day in 14 months as its stock [craters 6%](https://x.com/Barchart/status/2070397564927524889?s=20) after announcing [price increases](https://x.com/BetaProfiles/status/2070142975930462602?s=20) across the board — Microsoft falls to [lowest closing price](https://x.com/Barchart/status/2070368160465703241?s=20) since November 2023

* [US PCE inflation](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2070129534087610551?s=20) rises to 3 year high

* South Korea’s Kospi index [falls 8%](https://x.com/Barchart/status/2070364469104894222?s=20) triggering circuit breakers — Nikkei [falls 5%](https://x.com/Barchart/status/2070377380619633016?s=20)

* Binance will [stop serving EU clients](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070391384041717955?s=20) next week after they fail to secure a MiCA license

* Spot Bitcoin ETFs see almost [$700M in net outflows](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070414189693173817?s=20) as Bitcoin falls as low as [$58k](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070146589059092664?s=20)

* [USDT flips ETH](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070394184494989694?s=20) as the second largest crypto token by market cap

* $3M in Polymarket user [assets stolen](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070307673481097678?s=20) following a third party breach

* Draft Kings [announces DKEX](https://x.com/DegenerateNews/status/2070477053867012238?s=20) prediction market exchange

* Hyperliquid gets added to Singapore’s MAS [investor alert list](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070465447435133429?s=20)

* BitGo lays off [15% of staff](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070318865905762717?s=20) to shift focus to AI infra and stablecoins

* Kraken in talks to [acquire Aave](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2070216142942323141?s=20) at a $385M valuation — AAVE is up over 15% on the week and trading at a [$1.3B FDV](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/aave/)

* Number of

Fear and Greed is at
15
(Extreme
Fear)
and BTC is at
$59K
this morning after dropping to $58K yesterday. The specific
trigger was the PCE inflation reading for May, which came in at
4.1% year-over-year, the highest in three years, sending the
Nasdaq down 2% within 30 minutes and triggering over $600 million
in crypto liquidations in a single hour.

This is no longer a slow grind lower. This is a named event with
a named consequence. The market cap has dropped below $2.1T.
Strategy preferred shares hit an all-time low. And analysts are
now debating publicly whether $55K is next or whether this is the
capitulation floor.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Multicoin Just Set a Bold Target for HYPE by 2028

Multicoin Capital projected HYPE could reach approximately
$319 by 2028, more than 400% from current levels. The firm said
the market is pricing HYPE too narrowly as a crypto derivatives
exchange rather than a broader multi-asset platform.

Multicoin said HYPE is now one of its largest holdings.

What annual earnings figure does Multicoin use to arrive at its
price target?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/pqsNv-bDrpCFxeXH_xHQKg~~/AAQRxRA~/B0IacHrhgCjsR5eo3E_1fp3KKNgJugcGpPcM_WSf2vpaZFR-rvzr891lFgJBGwaNPY0wmd8nkwcvmzYuZNHyio4nLZerLBhauLmMpRExCX-iQWk2FkLJ_jADG5yg8s7yKScsCJN4YOOb0wUK2WH6v56Jf1TRyTXBMSaEIHonP8s6XKZObPiIrLwKj-HHYf0ITp5-xBZOPtHS6_qazU3vGoYla9UKv_O
